definition: Wishing to do what is right; careful and painstaking.
sentence_context: She is a conscientious student who always finishes her work.

definition: A word that sounds like what it means.
sentence_context: Buzz and hiss are examples of onomatopoeia.

definition: The study of the origin of words.
sentence_context: The etymology of robot comes from a Czech word.

definition: A period of new growth or interest.
sentence_context: The city is experiencing a cultural renaissance.

definition: A system where people are ranked one above another.
sentence_context: The company has a clear hierarchy.

definition: A movement requiring skill and care.
sentence_context: The pilot performed a difficult maneuver.

definition: A list of questions for a survey.
sentence_context: Please fill out this questionnaire.

definition: A strong, regular, repeated pattern of sound.
sentence_context: The song has a very catchy rhythm.

definition: A dark shape seen against a light background.
sentence_context: I saw the silhouette of a tree against the moon.

definition: A place to live or stay.
sentence_context: We need to find accommodation for the night.

definition: A complex system of rules and processes.
sentence_context: The government is often slow due to bureaucracy.

definition: Mutual trust and friendship among people.
sentence_context: There was a great sense of camaraderie in the team.

definition: To become progressively worse.
sentence_context: The weather began to deteriorate in the afternoon.

definition: A person who starts a business.
sentence_context: The young entrepreneur opened a new shop.

definition: A mode of behavior peculiar to an individual.
sentence_context: One of his idiosyncrasies was wearing mismatched socks.

definition: Two things being seen or placed close together with contrasting effect.
sentence_context: The juxtaposition of old and new buildings was striking.

definition: The process of keeping something in good condition.
sentence_context: The car needs regular maintenance.

definition: A system of naming things.
sentence_context: Chemical nomenclature can be very complex.

definition: An incident or event.
sentence_context: Snow is a rare occurrence in this part of the world.

definition: Present, appearing, or found everywhere.
sentence_context: Computers are ubiquitous in modern life.

definition: A group of islands.
sentence_context: The archipelago is located in the Pacific Ocean.

definition: The action or offense of speaking sacrilegiously about God or sacred things.
sentence_context: In some cultures, that would be considered blasphemy.

definition: A harsh discordant mixture of sounds.
sentence_context: A cacophony of car horns filled the city street.

definition: Shedding its leaves annually.
sentence_context: Maple trees are deciduous trees.

definition: Lasting for a very short time.
sentence_context: Fashions are often ephemeral.

definition: Treating serious issues with deliberately inappropriate humor.
sentence_context: He made a facetious remark during the meeting.

definition: Excessively talkative, especially on trivial matters.
sentence_context: He became more garrulous after a few drinks.

definition: Leadership or dominance, especially by one country or social group over others.
sentence_context: The country sought to establish its hegemony in the region.

definition: Just begun and so not fully formed or developed.
sentence_context: The project is still in its inchoate stages.

definition: Tending to talk a great deal; talkative.
sentence_context: She is a loquacious person who can talk for hours.

definition: Generous or forgiving, especially toward a rival or less powerful person.
sentence_context: He was magnanimous in victory.

definition: In the form of a cloud or haze; hazy.
sentence_context: His plans for the future were somewhat nebulous.

definition: Obedient or attentive to an excessive or servile degree.
sentence_context: The waiters were almost embarrassingly obsequious.

definition: A typical example or pattern of something; a model.
sentence_context: This is a new paradigm for education.

definition: Representing the most perfect or typical example of a quality or class.
sentence_context: He was the quintessential English gentleman.

definition: Say that one no longer holds an opinion or belief.
sentence_context: He was forced to recant his testimony.

definition: A person who acts obsequiously toward someone important in order to gain advantage.
sentence_context: I am not a sycophant for the boss.

definition: Reserved or uncommunicative in speech; saying little.
sentence_context: He was a taciturn man who rarely spoke.

definition: Find fault with someone; scold.
sentence_context: The teacher upbraided the student for being late.

definition: Alternate or waver between different opinions or actions; be indecisive.
sentence_context: I tend to vacillate between two choices.
